rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Avoid placing assume/guarantee statements in implementation scopes; they must be in component types.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced features and variables are declared in the same annex block.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations.
Validate that expression types match between left and right sides of binary operations.
Remove invalid or unresolved clauses rather than adding missing architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee statements are only allowed in component type scopes, not in implementations.
Type mismatches in expressions should be resolved by aligning helper declaration types with their expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing references by introducing new architecture elements not present in the model.
Avoid placing component type contracts (assume/guarantee) in implementation-level annexes.
Do not use invalid or non-standard AGREE scalar type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'.
